target.name: Loihichelin C
target.motivation: To obtain insights into the Fe(III)-chelating and Mn(II)-oxidizing functions of Loihichelin C (1a), and to understand the structural basis for Mn(III) stabilization and Mn(II) oxidation by synthesizing 1a and two analogs (1b and 1c) with stereochemically inverted and deleted hydroxy groups, respectively.

success_criteria.free_text: Achieve efficient solid-phase synthesis of 1a-1c, allowing for functional assessment of Fe(III)/Mn(III)-binding and Mn(II)-oxidizing activities, and elucidation of structure-function relationships.

key_disconnections.reasoning: Operational simplicity in stepwise elongation, structural diversification, and purification.

key_disconnections.bond_description: amide bond between ahOrn-5 and ΔAbu-6
key_disconnections.reasoning: The chemically unstable alkenyl amine cannot be used for direct amidation, necessitating a method for stereoselective construction of enamides.

retrosynthetic_strategy.rationale: A solid-phase strategy was chosen for its efficiency in assembling the complex peptidic structure. Orthogonal protecting groups (Fmoc for N-alpha, acid-labile trityl for solid support, Boc/Tr/t-Bu for side chains) were employed. The unstable alkenyl amine required a stereoretentive Staudinger ligation for the ΔAbu-6 moiety. The C-terminal chOrn-8 used an Alloc group to prevent Fmoc removal during resin loading.

thought.background: Loihichelin C (1a) is an 8-mer peptidic natural product with unusual nonproteinogenic amino acids. Its synthesis requires careful handling of stereochemistry and protecting groups.
thought.gap: How to efficiently assemble the complex peptide sequence, especially the unstable ΔAbu-6 and the C-terminal chOrn-8, and ensure orthogonality of protecting groups.
thought.decision: To achieve the total synthesis of 1a-1c, a solid-phase strategy will be adopted. The peptide will be disassembled into eight components, with 2, 3, 4a, and 4b requiring synthetic preparation. N-alpha-Fmoc groups will be used for the main chain, acid-labile trityl as the solid-phase linker, and Boc, Tr, or t-Bu groups for side-chain polar functionalities. The N-alpha-Alloc group will be used for C-terminal chOrn-8 (3) to avoid Fmoc removal during loading. A stereoretentive traceless Staudinger ligation will be used for dipeptide 2 (ahOrn-5-ΔAbu-6).

evidence.text_span: Due to its operational simplicity in stepwise elongation, structural diversification, and purification, we adopted a solid-phase strategy for total syntheses of loihichelin C (1a, Figure 1a), epimeric analog 1b, and deoxy analog 1c. 21,22 To ensure the orthogonality with base-labile Nα-Fmoc groups of the main α chain,23,24 the acid-labile trityl group was to be utilized as the solid-phase linker, and Boc, Tr, or t-Bu groups were to cap the polar functionalities of the side-chains.25 Accordingly, 1a−1c were retrosynthetically disassembled into eight components, among which 2, 3, 4a, and 4b necessitated synthetic preparation (Figure 1c). Nα-Fmoc-protected dipeptide 2 was designed to possess the Boc group at the N-hydroxy-N-acetamide moiety of ahOrn-5 and the Z-configured enamide of ΔAbu-6. Introducing a bulky t-Bu group to the side-chain carboxylic acids of 4a and 4b26 was expected to provide kinetic protection for their carbonyl groups and the adjacent unprotected hydroxy groups against undesired reactions. The Nα-Alloc group of 3 was selected for C-terminal chOrn-8, as a previous study indicated undesired Fmoc removal during base-induced loading onto a solid support.27

thought.background: Dipeptide 2, containing the ΔAbu-6 residue, is difficult to synthesize via direct amidation due to the instability of alkenyl amine.
thought.gap: How to stereoselectively construct the Z-configured enamide of ΔAbu-6.
thought.decision: To synthesize dipeptide 2, a stereoretentive traceless Staudinger ligation will be employed between phosphinophenol ester 5 and Z-alkenyl azide 6. A structurally optimized triarylphosphine (5) bearing two trifluoromethyl groups and one methoxy group will be used to facilitate stereoselective enamide construction.

outcome.observation: Enamide 7 was stereoselectively generated in 67% yield.

evidence.text_span: Unsaturate d dipeptide 2 was stereoselectively constructed from phosphinophenol ester 5 and Z-alkenyl azide 6 by a stereoretentive traceless Staudinger ligation,29−32 because the chemically unstable alkenyl amine cannot be used for direct amidation (Scheme 1). The triarylphosphine of 5 bearing two trifluoromethyl groups and one methoxy group was structurally optimized in our laboratory to facilitate the stereoselective construction of enamides.27,33,34 Thus, 5 and 6 were individually prepared and then incubated in aqueous 1,4- dioxane at 15 °C, stereoselectively generating enamide 7 in 67% yield.35

thought.background: The phosphinophenol ester 5 was optimized for stereoselective enamide construction.
thought.gap: Is the optimized phosphinophenol ester crucial for high yield?
thought.decision: A less optimized, unsubstituted phosphinophenol ester was used for comparison.

outcome.observation: When the corresponding unsubstituted phosphinophenol ester was used for the ligation, the yield of 7 was decreased to 38%.

evidence.text_span: When the corresponding unsubstituted phosphinophenol ester was used for the ligation, the yield of 7 was decreased to 38% demonstrating the superiority of the CF3/OMe-substituted phosphinophenol ester. See Supporting Information for details.

thought.gap: The allyl group of 7 needs to be removed to obtain the desired dipeptide 2.
thought.decision: To remove the allyl group, Pd(0)-mediated deprotection will be performed using Pd(PPh3)4 and morpholine.

outcome.observation: The allyl group of 7 was removed, giving rise to the requisite dipeptide 2 in 89% yield.

evidence.text_span: The allyl group of 7 was in turn removed by the action of Pd(PPh3)4 and morpholine, giving rise to the requisite dipeptide 2.

thought.background: All necessary components (2, 3, 4a, 4b, and commercial amino acids) are in hand.
thought.gap: The first step of solid-phase synthesis is to load the C-terminal amino acid (3) onto the resin (8).
thought.decision: To load N-alpha-Alloc-protected 3 onto trityl-OH ChemMatrix resin 8, the resin will first be chlorinated with SOCl2, then the sterically hindered N-hydroxy group of 3 will be reacted with the chlorotrityl resin in the presence of MTBD in MeCN at 50 °C.

evidence.text_span: With all the components in hand, we initiated the total syntheses of 1a−1c by appending Nα-Alloc-protected 3 to trityl-OH ChemMatrix36 resin 8 (Scheme 2). After chlorination of the resin with SOCl2, the sterically hindered N-hydroxy group of 3 was submitted to the resultant chlorotrityl resin and 7-methyl-1,5,7-triazabicyclo[4.4.0]dec-5-ene (MTBD) in MeCN at 50 °C to produce 9.

thought.gap: The N-alpha-Alloc group needs to be removed, and the next amino acid, Fmoc-D-Ser(t-Bu)-OH, needs to be coupled.
thought.decision: To elongate the peptide chain, Pd(0)-mediated removal of the N-alpha-Alloc group will be followed by condensation of the N-terminal amine with Fmoc-D-Ser(t-Bu)-OH using HATU and HOAt at 40 °C under microwave irradiation.

outcome.observation: Resin-bound dipeptide 10 was obtained in 51% yield over two steps.

evidence.text_span: Pd(0)-mediated removal of the Nα-Alloc group and ensuing condensation of the Nterminal amine with Fmoc-D-Ser(t-Bu)-OH using O-(7- azabenzotriazol-1-yl)-N,N,N′,N′-tetramethyluronium hexafluorophosphate (HATU) and 1-hydroxy-7-azabenzotriazole (HOAt)37 at 40 °C under microwave irradiation38 led to resinbound dipeptide 10.

thought.gap: The next amino acid, unsaturated dipeptide 2, needs to be coupled without affecting its Z-configuration.
thought.decision: To couple dipeptide 2, the N-terminal Fmoc group will be removed with piperidine, followed by introduction of unsaturated dipeptide 2 using PyBOP and HOAt at 40 °C.

outcome.observation: Tetrapeptide 11 was provided without affecting the Z-configuration of ΔAbu-6.

evidence.text_span: Removal of the N-terminal Fmoc group with piperidine, followed by introduction of unsaturated d i p e p t i d e 2 u s i n g ( b e n z o t r i a z o l - 1 - y l o x y ) - tripyrrolidinophosphonium hexafluorophosphate (PyBOP)39,40 (PyBOP)39,40 and HOAt at 40 °C, provided tetrapeptide 11 without affecting the Z-configuration of ΔAbu-6.

thought.gap: The remaining three amino acid residues need to be condensed to form the heptapeptide.
thought.decision: To complete the heptapeptide, the Fmoc removal and HATU/HOAt-mediated amidation steps will be repeated for the three amino acid residues.

evidence.text_span: The three amino acid residues were then condensed with 11 by repeating the Fmoc removal and HATU/HOAt-mediated amidation to furnish heptapeptide 12.

thought.background: The OHAsp residue is crucial for the target molecule's function.
thought.gap: How to protect the OHAsp residue during synthesis to prevent undesired side reactions.
thought.decision: Initially, an acetonide-protected OHAsp residue was employed for total synthesis of 1a.

outcome.observation: Only a trace amount of 1a was obtained, and the major product was the corresponding dehydrated aspartimide.

evidence.text_span: Initially, an acetonide-protected OHAsp residue was employed for total synthesis of 1a. Only a trace amount of 1a was obtained, however, and the major product was the corresponding dehydrated aspartimide. See Supporting Information for details.

thought.background: Acetonide protection of OHAsp led to dehydration and aspartimide formation.
thought.gap: A more suitable protecting group strategy is needed for OHAsp.
thought.decision: To prevent undesired reactions, a bulky t-Bu group will be introduced to the side-chain carboxylic acids of 4a and 4b to provide kinetic protection for their carbonyl groups and the adjacent unprotected hydroxy groups.

outcome.observation: This revised strategy was successful in preventing dehydration and aspartimide formation, leading to the successful synthesis of 1a-1c.

evidence.text_span: Introducing a bulky t-Bu group to the side-chain carboxylic acids of 4a and 4b26 was expected to provide kinetic protection for their carbonyl groups and the adjacent unprotected hydroxy groups against undesired reactions.

thought.background: Heptapeptide 12 has been synthesized, and the route now diverges for 1a, 1b, and 1c.
thought.gap: The final amino acid (OHAsp-1 or its analogs) and the N-terminal DDA need to be attached, followed by deprotection and purification.
thought.decision: After N-alpha-deprotection of 12, 4a (for 1a), 4b (for 1b), and Fmoc-D-Asp(t-Bu)-OH (for 1c) will be separately attached using HATU/HOAt. The Fmoc groups will then be detached, and (Z)-dodec-5-enoic acid will be amidated using HATU/HOAt to afford the fully protected resin-bound octapeptides 13a, 13b, and 13c. Finally, all acid-labile groups and the trityl resin will be removed with aqueous trifluoroacetic acid.

outcome.observation: Fully protected resin-bound octapeptides 13a, 13b, and 13c were obtained, and subsequent deprotection yielded crude 1a, 1b, and 1c.

evidence.text_span: The route diverged from common intermediate 12 for the syntheses of three target compounds 1a, 1b, and 1c. Namely, after Nα-deprotection of 12, 4a, 4b, and its deoxy analog (Fmoc-D-Asp(t-Bu)-OH) were separately attached using HATU/HOAt, and the Fmoc groups of the three adducts were detached using piperidine. Subsequently, the HATU/ HOAt reagent system was applied to the amidation between (Z)-dodec-5-enoic acid and the three amines to afford the fully protected resin-bound octapeptides 13a, 13b, and 13c. The last treatment with aqueous trifluoroacetic acid removed all acid-labile groups (t-Bu, Boc, and Tr) as well as the trityl resin.

thought.background: Crude 1a, 1b, and 1c were obtained after deprotection.
thought.gap: Purification of 1a-1c is challenging due to their metal-chelating properties.

outcome.observation: Purification of the thus-obtained 1a, 1b, and 1c, however, was not straightforward. Because of the presence of the carboxylate and hydroxamates, 1a−1c readily captured a trace amount of aluminum ions from borosilicate glassware, generating the corresponding Al(III) complexes.

evidence.text_span: Purification of the thus-obtained 1a, 1b, and 1c, however, was not straightforward. Because of the presence of the carboxylate and hydroxamates, 1a−1c readily captured a trace amount of aluminum ions from borosilicate glassware, generating the corresponding Al(III) complexes.

thought.background: 1a-1c readily form Al(III) complexes, hindering purification.
thought.gap: A method is needed to remove aluminum ions and obtain pure, metal-free compounds.
thought.decision: To isolate 1a as an aluminum-free form, an excess amount of more Lewis acidic GaCl3 in water will be utilized to convert the 1a-Al(III) complex to the 1a-Ga(III) complex, which will then be treated with EDTA-2Na. For 1b and 1c, direct application of EDTA will be used. All compounds will then be purified by HPLC with a reversed-phase column and a metal-free flow path, collecting fractions in quartz glassware.

outcome.observation: The first total synthesis of loihichelin C (1a) was achieved in 26% yield over 14 steps from 10. Alternatively, direct application of EDTA successfully removed an aluminum ion from 1b-Al(III) and 1c-Al(III), and the resultant 1b and 1c were subjected to HPLC purification to isolate 1b and 1c in 18% and 31% yields from 10, respectively. The high overall yields of all three compounds corroborated the robustness of our newly developed synthetic procedures for assembling these unusual sequences.

evidence.text_span: To isolate 1a as an aluminum-free form, an excess amount of more Lewis acidic GaCl3 in water was utilized to convert the 1a-Al(III) complex to the 1a-Ga(III) complex, which was then treated with a potent chelating agent, disodium ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid (EDTA-2Na). Finally, pure 1a was isolated by HPLC with a reversed-phase column and a metal-free flow path, collecting fractions in quartz glassware. Hence, the first total synthesis of loihichelin C (1a) was achieved in 26% yield over 14 steps from 10. Alternatively, direct application of EDTA successfully removed an aluminum ion from 1b-Al(III) and 1c-Al(III), and the resultant 1b and 1c were subjected to HPLC purification to isolate 1b and 1c in 18% and 31% yields from 10, respectively. The high overall yields of all three compounds corroborated the robustness of our newly developed synthetic procedures for assembling these unusual sequences.

validation.significance: The total synthesis of loihichelin C (1a) and its analogs (1b, 1c) enabled the first comprehensive functional analysis of its Fe(III)/Mn(III)-chelating and Mn(II)-oxidizing activities. It revealed that the properly oriented hydroxy group of OHAsp-1 in 1a is crucial for strong Fe(III) binding, stabilization of metastable Mn(III), and promotion of Mn(II)-to-Mn(III) oxidation. The study also uncovered an unusual Mn(III)-mediated degradation pathway for 1b and 1c, highlighting the unique protective role of 1a's structure. These findings provide a structural basis for Mn(III) stabilization and Mn(II) oxidation, suggesting intrinsic roles for 1a in bacterial iron acquisition and manganese metabolism in deep-sea vent environments.

source.title: Total Synthesis and Functional Analysis of Loihichelin C: Structural Basis for Mn(III) Stabilization and Mn(II) Oxidation.
source.authors: Sawada M, Kakizawa H, Takeuchi A, Itoh H, Inoue M.
